a208-1.description: E session cost trend at 4th consecutive breach ($1.95 last-5). wq-890 fix confirmed effective at A#208.
a208-1.resolution: E last-5 avg still $1.95. Pre-fix sessions have aged out — s1844=$2.27 is a POST-FIX session that violated both the $1.80 cost cap and the 6-minute duration gate (ran 10m00s). Post-fix E sessions: s1834=$1.45, s1839=$1.53, s1844=$2.27. Fix is NOT consistently effective — 1/3 post-fix sessions violated. Cost controls are advisory-only (post-hoc warnings), cannot stop a running session.

recommended_actions.description: E session cost controls are advisory-only and failing — s1844 violated both $1.80 cap and 6m duration gate (ran 10m00s, cost $2.27). Post-fix sessions inconsistent: 2/3 under cap, 1/3 over. 5th consecutive audit with E last-5 avg above $1.50 threshold.
recommended_actions.action: Investigate why s1844 ignored duration gate. Consider enforcing hard budget in E session pre-hook (inject absolute timeout via SESSION_ENGAGE.md) rather than relying on advisory post-hoc warnings. clawnews circuit-break (3 consecutive API_ERROR substitutions) should also be implemented to reduce E session complexity.
